mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals robust aggregate demand and business confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, real GDP growth becomes the primary driver of equity valuations; accelerating quarterly GDP growth compresses risk premiums and lifts forward returns.
failure_condition: The positive correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day SPX returns vanishes or turns negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding corporate revenue potential, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with consecutive months of PAYEMS growth (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When core consumer price inflation exceeds 3.0% annually, real purchasing power erodes and central bank tightening expectations rise, depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL annualized growth crosses above 3.0% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling real consumer credit availability signals a demand shock that rapidly deteriorates corporate earnings expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising UNRATE and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained housing construction, supply bottlenecks amplify the impact of industrial production growth on corporate margins, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The positive predictive power of INDPRO on 20-day SPX returns disappears or reverses when HOUST remains above 1500 units for three consecutive months.
